lede: A look at May 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Richard Wagner and Charles Aznavour, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2026 Liushenyu coal mine explosion
events.desc: A gas explosion at a coal mine in Changzhi, Shanxi, leaves at least 82 people dead.

events.title: Republic of Ireland
events.desc: The Republic of Ireland becomes the first nation in the world to utilise a public referendum to legalise gay marriage.

events.title: Prayut Chan-o-cha
events.desc: General Prayut Chan-o-cha becomes interim leader of Thailand in a military coup d'état, following six months of political turmoil.

events.title: Bosnia and Herzegovina
events.desc: Bosnia and Herzegovina, Croatia and Slovenia join the United Nations.

events.title: 1960 Valdivia earthquake
events.desc: The Great Chilean earthquake, measuring 9.5 on the moment magnitude scale, hits southern Chile, becoming the most powerful earthquake ever recorded.

events.title: 1958 anti-Tamil pogrom
events.desc: The 1958 riots in Ceylon become a watershed in the race relations of various ethnic communities of Sri Lanka. The total deaths are estimated at 300, mostly Tamils.

events.title: Lassen Peak
events.desc: Lassen Peak erupts with a powerful force, the only volcano besides Mount St. Helens to erupt in the contiguous U.S. during the 20th century.

events.title: HMS Beagle
events.desc: HMS Beagle departs on its first voyage.

events.title: 1766 Istanbul earthquake
events.desc: A large earthquake causes heavy damage and loss of life in Istanbul and the Marmara region.

events.title: Trevi Fountain
events.desc: Trevi Fountain is officially completed and inaugurated in Rome.

deaths.text: Victor Hugo, French novelist, poet, and playwright
Renata of Lorraine, Duchess of Bavaria from 1579 to 1597

observances.text: Aromanian National Day (marginal, celebration on May 23 is more common)
Christian feast day: Blessed Maria Domenica Brun Barbantini
Christian feast day: Michael Hồ Đình Hy (one of Vietnamese Martyrs)
